pr.title: PR #136 — Execution Readiness Framework v1
pr.description:
## Summary

Adds the **readiness** layer of the execution roadmap, on top of Constraint (#135). Where prior layers describe state / location / requirements / boundaries, this layer determines **whether execution can proceed** for a given `(context, environment, resource, constraint, capability)` tuple: its `readiness_status` plus the typed readiness `checks` that were evaluated. Observational/evaluative only — no execution, orchestration, scheduling, optimization, or worker assignment (per non-goals). Upstream layers are consumed read-only.

Same deterministic, append-only pattern as the rest of the family (`execution_constraint.py` is the direct template):

- **`ExecutionReadiness`** — `readiness_id, context_id, environment_id, resource_id, constraint_id, capability_id, readiness_status ∈ {READY, NOT_READY, DEGRADED, UNKNOWN}, checks[], summary, created_at, schema_version, raw_payload`
- **`ExecutionReadinessCheck`** — `check_id, check_type ∈ {CONTEXT_CHECK, ENVIRONMENT_CHECK, RESOURCE_CHECK, CONSTRAINT_CHECK, VALIDATION_CHECK, OTHER}, status, summary`
- **`ExecutionReadinessReport`** — `report_id, readiness_count, readiness_status_counts, check_type_counts, check_count, ready_count, degraded_count, not_ready_count, duplicate_readiness_count, created_at`
- **`ExecutionReadinessEvidence`** + **`ExecutionReadinessEngine`**

Behavior:
- **Normalization**: `readiness_status`, `check_type`, and check `status` upper-cased; unknown enum values rejected.
- **Deterministic ordering**: readinesses sorted by `(context_id, environment_id, resource_id, constraint_id, capability_id, readiness_status, readiness_id)`; checks by `(check_type, status, check_id)`.
- **Duplicate detection**: 5-tuple `(context_id, environment_id, resource_id, constraint_id, capability_id)`, keep first, count the rest.
- **Aggregation**: status counts + check-type counts (sorted); `ready/degraded/not_ready_count` derived from status counts.
- **Pass/fail**: `passed = readiness_count > 0 and not_ready_count == 0 and degraded_count == 0` (empty → fail).
- **Evidence bundle** always written under `artifacts/execution_readiness/<run_id>/`: `execution_readiness_request.json`, `execution_readiness_result.json`, `execution_readiness_summary.md`, `pass_fail.json` (+ `report.json`).

CLI (registered in the governed command registry, `READ_ONLY`/`SAFE`, `POETRY_ENV`):
`axiom execution-readiness-create | execution-readinesses | execution-readiness-show | execution-readiness-export` (JSON/Markdown/CSV).

### What did not change
2024 Revit baseline unaffected. No architecture changes. No network calls. Upstream context/environment/resource/constraint layers consumed read-only.

### Validation
- `poetry run pytest -q` → **4633 passed, 1 skipped** (+52; baseline 4581 at #135)
- `poetry run ruff check src/ tests/` → **0 errors**
- CLI smoke (adversarial fixture: 4 records, 1 case-insensitive duplicate, 1 DEGRADED, 1 NOT_READY, lowercase statuses/checks, out-of-order checks) → readiness_count 3 / duplicate 1 / ready 1 / degraded 1 / not_ready 1 / checks 3; sorted status + check-type counts; deterministic order.


Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
